tweets.full_text:
Solana TVL leaders have changed a lot

@kamino is still holding 2nd place and could overtake Jupiter soon

Why?

Institutional partnerships like @Figure + multiple lending vaults

That structure works in markets like this

Meanwhile @JupiterExchange launched its own stablecoin, but it adds only $55M to lending

No real institutional partnerships, no RWA
Most of their TVL still comes from JLP pool

Great in bull market
Different story in slow markets

Staking protocols like @jito_sol, @sanctumso, @doublezero have lost TVL as SOL price dropped

Overall Solana TVL went from $13.2B → $6.4B
Still ~10x smaller than Ethereum

This cycle is only about institutionals players, they bring big money

tweets.full_text:
Launch recap of @NOMADZxyz at the @Solana dApp Store.

We participated in the first Hackathon by @solanamobile and @RadiantsDAO.

Many builders were skeptical. Allocating resources to a new ecosystem felt risky.

We built a simple MVP, recorded a short demo, made a few posts, and shipped.

- 200+ teams applied.
- 10 projects won.

We were lucky to be one of them - huge credit to @petro_nomadz and @beeman_nl for the execution.

On January 14, we went live with support from the Solana Mobile team (@0x_diablo, @therealchaseeb, @CryptoMagellan) and @toly.

For builders who are still skeptical about participation in the second Solana Mobile Hackathon, called Monolith, I am attaching some of the metrics.
 
Screenshoot 1: @privy_io 

Over the past month, we’ve continued building steady momentum.

We now have 16,037 total users, with 3,839 active users and 1,777 new users joining recently. Nearly 40% of our user base came from launching the app.

Screenshoot 2: @solscanofficial 

From Jan 14–20, our Solana program processed roughly 8-9k total transactions, peaking at over 2k in a single day on Jan 14.​ Currently, a total of 35k transactions, ~45% came from the app launch.

Screenshoot 3: @cherrydotfun 

We crossed 1,155 total Nomadz reviews with an average rating of 4.54/5 and 86% positive sentiment.​

1,101 reviewers connected a wallet (95% verified), and the average balance of these users is about 1,702 USD, proving we’re reaching high-intent crypto travelers.​

Screenshot 4: X (Nomadz profile analytics)

In the first month after launch, the Nomadz profile generated 539.9K impressions, up 132%.

We had 29.9K total engagements (+214%) with an engagement rate reached 5.5% (+35%). We also saw 4.2K profile visits (+264%), indicating strong curiosity and conversion from content to brand exploration.

All of this started from a lightweight MVP and a decision to participate.

Now the second Solana Mobile Hackathon - Monolith is live.

If you’re a builder still hesitating:

This is an asymmetric upside.
Early distribution.
Real users.
Real on-chain traction.
Real visibility.

You don’t need a perfect product.
You need a working one.
Ship it.

Monolith is not just a hackathon - it’s a distribution gateway.

data.translation:
Bang, biasanya main 0.5 SOL, 5 menit cabut, rata-rata dapetnya 1-2$, mohon nasihatnya bang, supaya minimal bisa dapet 10$ tiap hari. 🙏

Bro, kalau cuma 0.5 SOL dan cuma 5 menit, 1-2$ itu udah lumayan bagus sih, artinya kamu masih di zona profit kecil tapi konsisten.

Tapi kalau targetnya 10$/hari, secara realistis kamu perlu naikkan salah satu (atau kombinasi) dari 3 hal ini:

1. Naikkan modal per trade (contoh: 1–2 SOL per trade)  
   → Kalau win rate & RR kamu bagus, profit per win langsung naik 2–4x lipat.

2. Naikkan frekuensi (main lebih lama atau lebih banyak sesi)  
   → Misal dari 5 menit jadi 15–30 menit per sesi, atau main 3–4 sesi sehari.

3. Tingkatkan win rate / risk-reward ratio  
   → Fokus cari setup yang lebih tajam (contoh: sniper bot + filter bagus, atau main di pair yang lagi panas).  
   → Kalau sekarang RR kamu cuma 1:1 atau 1:1.5, coba cari yang minimal 1:2 atau 1:3.

Contoh perhitungan kasar biar 10$/hari:  
- Modal 1.5 SOL per trade  
- Target profit per win 4–5$ (RR 1:2.5 atau lebih)  
- Win rate 40–50%  
- Main 4–6 kali sehari → cukup realistis buat 10–15$ kalau lagi kondisi pasar oke.

Tapi inget bang: naik modal & volume = naik risiko juga.  
Jangan all-in sekali gus, tetep pakai risk management (stoploss, jangan revenge trade, jangan FOMO).

Semangat bang, dari 1-2$ ke 10$ itu masih masuk akal kok, asal sabar naikin skill & modal pelan-pelan. 💪🙏

some people ask me the same question. when and how do i cut losses in LPs on @MeteoraAG

honest take, i rarely cut losses on LPs. but there's a plenty situation that you can do when you position on losses.

don't panic, don't immediately take the L. @Heavymetalcook6 said 

https://t.co/u7TkhO5H1j

- some people would cutting their losses after their position out of range for 30 minutes.
- some people would cutting their losses when the token had no chance to survive (don't do this)
- some people use maximum threshold, i.e. 15% loss and i'll cut
- some people never cut losses and become community member (not a good one too)
- some people wait until the support can't hold first thing you need to do is, find out why the token dumping.

check the x comm, check who's selling. if the community didn't believe then just cut loss.

check the pump candle on 5m tf, who's holding the floor and still hold. you can click on the candle and filter the buy amount with gmgn. 

if the people who holding the floor get out, then it's time to cut loss.

never cut loss on a first dump, wait until retrace if the floor still holds you can hold your positions.

yes this method isn't the best way to cut losses, but if i select a token to LPs i fully believe about what i pick.
